content: read reviews/956.md. if the summary section of the checklist review indicates the review passed, do nothing. if it failed, make relevant updates that are needed to deepmind/956.lean accordingly. if in doubt, reference CHECKLIST.md, which was the source document used when a previous instance of claude produced reviews/956.md.

    10‚Üí---

    26‚Üí- [x] Starts with a heading: `# ErdŇĎs Problem 956`.
    27‚Üí- [x] Contains a `*Reference:*` line with a markdown link to the source:
    28‚Üí  `*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/956](https://www.erdosproblems.com/956)`
    29‚Üí- [x] Includes a brief plain-English description of the problem (defines set distance őī, translates, h(n), etc.).
    30‚Üí- [x] Bibliography entry present: `[ErPa90] ErdŇĎs, P. and Pach, J.`
    31‚Üí
    32‚Üí## 4. Namespace
    33‚Üí
    34‚Üí- [x] All declarations are inside `namespace Erdos956 ... end Erdos956`.
    35‚Üí- [x] Namespace name uses `UpperCamelCase` (`Erdos956`).
    36‚Üí- [x] No `open` / `open scoped` statements are needed; none present. N/A.
    37‚Üí
    38‚Üí## 5. Custom Definitions
    39‚Üí
    40‚Üí- [x] `setDist956` has a LaTeX docstring.
    41‚Üí- [x] `setDist956` is marked `noncomputable` (uses `sInf`).
    42‚Üí- [x] `translate956` has a LaTeX docstring.
    43‚Üí- [x] `translate956` is not marked `noncomputable` (pure set image, computable). Correct.
    44‚Üí- [x] No `sorry` in definitions.
    45‚Üí- [x] Functions use `lowerCamelCase` (`setDist956`, `translate956`).
    46‚Üí- [x] No `local notation` needed; none present.
    47‚Üí
    48‚Üí> **Note (non-blocking):** Mathlib provides `Metric.infDist` (point-to-set) and `EMetric.infEdist` (set-to-set in extended metric). The custom `setDist956` is reasonable here since the standard Mathlib set-to-set distance API doesn't directly match the needed `‚ĄĚ`-valued `inf {dist c d | c ‚ąą C, d ‚ąą D}`. Similarly, `translate956` could use `Set.image_vadd` / `(¬∑ +ŠĶ• ¬∑)` but the `Add`-based definition is correct for `EuclideanSpace ‚ĄĚ (Fin 2)`.
    49‚Üí
    50‚Üí## 6. Theorem Statements
    51‚Üí
    52‚Üí- [x] Both `erdos_956` and `erdos_956.variants.upper_bound` use the `theorem` keyword.
    53‚Üí- [x] Main theorem named `erdos_956` (snake_case, matching namespace number).
    54‚Üí- [x] Names follow `snake_case` convention.
    55‚Üí- [x] Each theorem has both `@[category ...]` and `@[AMS ...]` on the **same attribute line**.
    56‚Üí- [x] Both theorems have docstrings.
    57‚Üí- [x] Docstrings reference `[ErPa90]`.
    58‚Üí
    59‚Üí### `answer()` usage
    60‚Üí
    61‚ÜíThe source problem says: "Prove that there exists a constant c>0 such that h(n) > n^{1+c} for all large n." This is a direct assertion (**Case 3**), so no `answer()` wrapper is needed.
    62‚Üí
    63‚Üí- [x] Correctly formalized as a direct assertion (Case 3): `‚ąÉ c : ‚ĄĚ, c > 0 ‚ąß ...` with no `answer()`.
    64‚Üí
    65‚Üí> **Note (non-blocking):** The source also asks to "Determine h(n)" (a Case 2 / find-the-value question). The formalization only captures the lower bound conjecture and the upper bound result, not the determination of h(n). This is acceptable ‚ÄĒ the concrete lower bound conjecture is the substantive open problem.
    66‚Üí
    67‚Üí## 7. Category Attribute
    68‚Üí
    69‚Üí- [x] `erdos_956`: `category research open` ‚ÄĒ correct, problem is open, proof uses `sorry`.
    70‚Üí- [x] `erdos_956.variants.upper_bound`: `category research solved` ‚ÄĒ correct, the upper bound O(n^{4/3}) was proved by ErdŇĎs and Pach.
    71‚Üí- [x] No duplicate or missing category attributes.
    72‚Üí
    73‚Üí## 8. AMS Attribute
    74‚Üí
    75‚Üí- [ ] **FAIL: AMS codes are not in ascending numeric order.** Both theorems use `AMS 52 5`, but 52 > 5. Should be `AMS 5 52`.
    76‚Üí- [x] At least one AMS subject number is listed (two: 5 and 52).
    77‚Üí- [x] Both 5 (Combinatorics) and 52 (Convex and discrete geometry) are valid MSC2020 codes and appropriate for this problem.
    78‚Üí- [x] No duplicate numbers.
    79‚Üí- [x] Exactly one `@[AMS ...]` attribute per theorem.
    80‚Üí

    83‚Üí- [x] `erdos_956` uses multi-line `by\n  sorry` ‚ÄĒ acceptable.
    84‚Üí- [x] `erdos_956.variants.upper_bound` uses multi-line `by\n  sorry` ‚ÄĒ acceptable.
    85‚Üí- [x] No `sorry` in `def` or `abbrev` declarations.
    86‚Üí- [x] No informal proof sketches for the open problem.
    87‚Üí- [x] No `native_decide`.
    88‚Üí
    89‚Üí## 10. Variants & Multi-part Problems
    90‚Üí
    91‚Üí- [x] Variant named `erdos_956.variants.upper_bound` (correct naming convention).
    92‚Üí- [x] Variant is in the same file as the main theorem.
    93‚Üí- [x] Variant has its own `@[category ..., AMS ...]` attributes.
    94‚Üí- [x] Variant has a docstring explaining it is the upper bound O(n^{4/3}).
    95‚Üí
    96‚Üí## 11. Code Quality

   100‚Üí- [x] No debug code (`#check`, `#eval`, `#print`, `dbg_trace`).
   101‚Üí- [x] No `set_option` disabling linters.
   102‚Üí- [x] Lines are reasonably short (all under 100 characters).
   103‚Üí- [x] File ends with a single trailing newline.
   104‚Üí

The definitions of set distance and translate are correct, the main theorem faithfully captures the lower bound conjecture (‚ąÉ c > 0, h(n) > n^{1+c} for large n), and the variant correctly states the known upper bound h(n) = O(n^{4/3}). Docstrings, namespace, copyright, and code style are all compliant.

# Formal Conjectures PR Checklist ‚ÄĒ `deepmind/956.lean`

Every item must pass before a PR is approved. Reviewers: reject if any box is unchecked
without an explicit justification.

Sources: [`formal-conjectures/README.md`](../formal-conjectures/README.md) (Style Guidelines ¬ß1‚Äď7),
[`AGENTS.md`](../formal-conjectures/AGENTS.md), and the five custom linters in
`FormalConjectures/Util/`.

---

## 1. Copyright Header

- [x] File begins with the **exact** copyright block (no leading blank line).
- [x] Year is exactly `2026`.

## 2. Import Statement

- [x] Exactly one import line, immediately after the copyright block (separated by one blank line):
  `import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports`
- [x] No other imports are present.

## 3. Module Docstring

- [x] A `/-! ... -/` module docstring appears after the import, separated by one blank line.
- [x] Starts with a heading: `# ErdŇĎs Problem 956`.
- [x] Contains a `*Reference:*` line with a markdown link to the source:
  `*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/956](https://www.erdosproblems.com/956)`
- [x] Includes a brief plain-English description of the problem (defines set distance őī, translates, h(n), etc.).
- [x] Bibliography entry present: `[ErPa90] ErdŇĎs, P. and Pach, J.`

## 4. Namespace

- [x] All declarations are inside `namespace Erdos956 ... end Erdos956`.
- [x] Namespace name uses `UpperCamelCase` (`Erdos956`).
- [x] No `open` / `open scoped` statements are needed; none present. N/A.

## 5. Custom Definitions

- [x] `setDist956` has a LaTeX docstring.
- [x] `setDist956` is marked `noncomputable` (uses `sInf`).
- [x] `translate956` has a LaTeX docstring.
- [x] `translate956` is not marked `noncomputable` (pure set image, computable). Correct.
- [x] No `sorry` in definitions.
- [x] Functions use `lowerCamelCase` (`setDist956`, `translate956`).
- [x] No `local notation` needed; none present.

> **Note (non-blocking):** Mathlib provides `Metric.infDist` (point-to-set) and `EMetric.infEdist` (set-to-set in extended metric). The custom `setDist956` is reasonable here since the standard Mathlib set-to-set distance API doesn't directly match the needed `‚ĄĚ`-valued `inf {dist c d | c ‚ąą C, d ‚ąą D}`. Similarly, `translate956` could use `Set.image_vadd` / `(¬∑ +ŠĶ• ¬∑)` but the `Add`-based definition is correct for `EuclideanSpace ‚ĄĚ (Fin 2)`.

## 6. Theorem Statements

- [x] Both `erdos_956` and `erdos_956.variants.upper_bound` use the `theorem` keyword.
- [x] Main theorem named `erdos_956` (snake_case, matching namespace number).
- [x] Names follow `snake_case` convention.
- [x] Each theorem has both `@[category ...]` and `@[AMS ...]` on the **same attribute line**.
- [x] Both theorems have docstrings.
- [x] Docstrings reference `[ErPa90]`.

### `answer()` usage

The source problem says: "Prove that there exists a constant c>0 such that h(n) > n^{1+c} for all large n." This is a direct assertion (**Case 3**), so no `answer()` wrapper is needed.

- [x] Correctly formalized as a direct assertion (Case 3): `‚ąÉ c : ‚ĄĚ, c > 0 ‚ąß ...` with no `answer()`.

> **Note (non-blocking):** The source also asks to "Determine h(n)" (a Case 2 / find-the-value question). The formalization only captures the lower bound conjecture and the upper bound result, not the determination of h(n). This is acceptable ‚ÄĒ the concrete lower bound conjecture is the substantive open problem.

## 7. Category Attribute

- [x] `erdos_956`: `category research open` ‚ÄĒ correct, problem is open, proof uses `sorry`.
- [x] `erdos_956.variants.upper_bound`: `category research solved` ‚ÄĒ correct, the upper bound O(n^{4/3}) was proved by ErdŇĎs and Pach.
- [x] No duplicate or missing category attributes.

## 8. AMS Attribute

- [ ] **FAIL: AMS codes are not in ascending numeric order.** Both theorems use `AMS 52 5`, but 52 > 5. Should be `AMS 5 52`.
- [x] At least one AMS subject number is listed (two: 5 and 52).
- [x] Both 5 (Combinatorics) and 52 (Convex and discrete geometry) are valid MSC2020 codes and appropriate for this problem.
- [x] No duplicate numbers.
- [x] Exactly one `@[AMS ...]` attribute per theorem.

## 9. Proof Bodies

- [x] `erdos_956` uses multi-line `by\n  sorry` ‚ÄĒ acceptable.
- [x] `erdos_956.variants.upper_bound` uses multi-line `by\n  sorry` ‚ÄĒ acceptable.
- [x] No `sorry` in `def` or `abbrev` declarations.
- [x] No informal proof sketches for the open problem.
- [x] No `native_decide`.

## 10. Variants & Multi-part Problems

- [x] Variant named `erdos_956.variants.upper_bound` (correct naming convention).
- [x] Variant is in the same file as the main theorem.
- [x] Variant has its own `@[category ..., AMS ...]` attributes.
- [x] Variant has a docstring explaining it is the upper bound O(n^{4/3}).

## 11. Code Quality

- [x] 2-space indentation throughout.
- [x] Unicode math symbols used (`‚ąÄ`, `‚ąÉ`, `‚ąą`, `‚ĄĚ`, `‚Ąē`, `‚Č•`, `‚Č†`, `‚Č§`, `‚Ąā` etc.).
- [x] No debug code (`#check`, `#eval`, `#print`, `dbg_trace`).
- [x] No `set_option` disabling linters.
- [x] Lines are reasonably short (all under 100 characters).
- [x] File ends with a single trailing newline.

## 12. Build Verification

- [ ] `lake build` ‚ÄĒ not verified in this review.
- [ ] All five custom linters pass ‚ÄĒ not verified (but `AMS 52 5` ordering will likely trigger `linter.style.ams_attribute`).

---

## Summary

**Status: NEEDS FIX**

One blocking issue found:

| # | Issue | Severity | Fix |
|---|-------|----------|-----|
| 1 | `AMS 52 5` ‚ÄĒ codes not in ascending order | **Blocking** | Change to `AMS 5 52` on both theorems (lines 55, 79) |

The formalization itself is mathematically sound. The definitions of set distance and translate are correct, the main theorem faithfully captures the lower bound conjecture (‚ąÉ c > 0, h(n) > n^{1+c} for large n), and the variant correctly states the known upper bound h(n) = O(n^{4/3}). Docstrings, namespace, copyright, and code style are all compliant.

1‚Üí/-
     2‚ÜíCopyright 2026 The Formal Conjectures Authors.
     3‚Üí
     4‚ÜíLicensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
     5‚Üíyou may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
     6‚ÜíYou may obtain a copy of the License at
     7‚Üí
     8‚Üí    https://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
     9‚Üí
    10‚ÜíUnless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
    11‚Üídistributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
    12‚ÜíWITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
    13‚ÜíSee the License for the specific language governing permissions and
    14‚Üílimitations under the License.
    15‚Üí-/
    16‚Üí
    17‚Üíimport FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports
    18‚Üí
    19‚Üí/-!
    20‚Üí# ErdŇĎs Problem 956
    21‚Üí
    22‚Üí*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/956](https://www.erdosproblems.com/956)
    23‚Üí
    24‚ÜíIf $C, D \subseteq \mathbb{R}^2$ then the distance between $C$ and $D$ is defined by
    25‚Üí$\delta(C, D) = \inf \{ \|c - d\| \mid c \in C, d \in D \}$.
    26‚Üí
    27‚ÜíLet $h(n)$ be the maximal number of unit distances between disjoint convex
    28‚Üítranslates. That is, the maximal $m$ such that there is a compact convex set
    29‚Üí$C \subset \mathbb{R}^2$ and a set $X$ of size $n$ such that all $(C + x)_{x \in X}$ are disjoint
    30‚Üíand there are $m$ pairs $x_1, x_2 \in X$ such that $\delta(C + x_1, C + x_2) = 1$.
    31‚Üí
    32‚Üí[ErPa90] ErdŇĎs, P. and Pach, J.
    33‚Üí-/
    34‚Üí
    35‚Üínamespace Erdos956
    36‚Üí
    37‚Üí/-- The distance between two subsets of a metric space:
    38‚Üí$\delta(C, D) = \inf \{ \operatorname{dist}(c, d) \mid c \in C, d \in D \}$. -/
    39‚Üínoncomputable def setDist956 {őĪ : Type*} [PseudoMetricSpace őĪ]
    40‚Üí    (C D : Set őĪ) : ‚ĄĚ :=
    41‚Üí  sInf {r : ‚ĄĚ | ‚ąÉ c ‚ąą C, ‚ąÉ d ‚ąą D, r = dist c d}
    42‚Üí
    43‚Üí/-- The translate of a set $C$ by a vector $x$: $C + x = \{ c + x \mid c \in C \}$. -/
    44‚Üídef translate956 {őĪ : Type*} [Add őĪ] (C : Set őĪ) (x : őĪ) : Set őĪ :=
    45‚Üí  (¬∑ + x) '' C
    46‚Üí
    47‚Üí/--
    48‚Üí**ErdŇĎs Problem 956** [ErPa90]:
    49‚Üí
    50‚ÜíThere exists a constant $c > 0$ such that for all sufficiently large $n$,
    51‚Üíone can find a compact convex set $C \subset \mathbb{R}^2$ and $n$ translation vectors whose
    52‚Üítranslates of $C$ are pairwise disjoint with more than $n^{1+c}$ pairs at
    53‚Üíunit set-distance.
    54‚Üí-/
    55‚Üí@[category research open, AMS 52 5]
    56‚Üítheorem erdos_956 :
    57‚Üí    ‚ąÉ c : ‚ĄĚ, c > 0 ‚ąß
    58‚Üí    ‚ąÉ N‚āÄ : ‚Ąē, ‚ąÄ n : ‚Ąē, n ‚Č• N‚āÄ ‚Üí
    59‚Üí      ‚ąÉ (C : Set (EuclideanSpace ‚ĄĚ (Fin 2))),
    60‚Üí        IsCompact C ‚ąß Convex ‚ĄĚ C ‚ąß C.Nonempty ‚ąß
    61‚Üí      ‚ąÉ (X : Finset (EuclideanSpace ‚ĄĚ (Fin 2))),
    62‚Üí        X.card = n ‚ąß
    63‚Üí        (‚ąÄ x‚āĀ ‚ąą X, ‚ąÄ x‚āā ‚ąą X, x‚āĀ ‚Č† x‚āā ‚Üí
    64‚Üí          Disjoint (translate956 C x‚āĀ) (translate956 C x‚āā)) ‚ąß
    65‚Üí      ‚ąÉ (P : Finset (EuclideanSpace ‚ĄĚ (Fin 2) √ó EuclideanSpace ‚ĄĚ (Fin 2))),
    66‚Üí        (‚ąÄ p ‚ąą P, p.1 ‚ąą X ‚ąß p.2 ‚ąą X ‚ąß p.1 ‚Č† p.2 ‚ąß
    67‚Üí          setDist956 (translate956 C p.1) (translate956 C p.2) = 1) ‚ąß
    68‚Üí        (‚ąÄ p ‚ąą P, (p.2, p.1) ‚ąČ P) ‚ąß
    69‚Üí        ((P.card : ‚ĄĚ) > (n : ‚ĄĚ) ^ ((1 : ‚ĄĚ) + c)) := by
    70‚Üí  sorry
    71‚Üí
    72‚Üí/--
    73‚Üí**ErdŇĎs Problem 956, upper bound** [ErPa90]:
    74‚Üí
    75‚Üí$h(n) \ll n^{4/3}$. For any compact convex set $C$ and $n$ translations with
    76‚Üípairwise disjoint translates, the number of pairs at unit set-distance
    77‚Üíis $O(n^{4/3})$.
    78‚Üí-/
    79‚Üí@[category research solved, AMS 52 5]
    80‚Üítheorem erdos_956.variants.upper_bound :
    81‚Üí    ‚ąÉ K : ‚ĄĚ, K > 0 ‚ąß
    82‚Üí    ‚ąÄ (C : Set (EuclideanSpace ‚ĄĚ (Fin 2))),
    83‚Üí      IsCompact C ‚Üí Convex ‚ĄĚ C ‚Üí C.Nonempty ‚Üí
    84‚Üí    ‚ąÄ (X : Finset (EuclideanSpace ‚ĄĚ (Fin 2))),
    85‚Üí      (‚ąÄ x‚āĀ ‚ąą X, ‚ąÄ x‚āā ‚ąą X, x‚āĀ ‚Č† x‚āā ‚Üí
    86‚Üí        Disjoint (translate956 C x‚āĀ) (translate956 C x‚āā)) ‚Üí
    87‚Üí    ‚ąÄ (P : Finset (EuclideanSpace ‚ĄĚ (Fin 2) √ó EuclideanSpace ‚ĄĚ (Fin 2))),
    88‚Üí      (‚ąÄ p ‚ąą P, p.1 ‚ąą X ‚ąß p.2 ‚ąą X ‚ąß p.1 ‚Č† p.2 ‚ąß
    89‚Üí        setDist956 (translate956 C p.1) (translate956 C p.2) = 1) ‚Üí
    90‚Üí      (‚ąÄ p ‚ąą P, (p.2, p.1) ‚ąČ P) ‚Üí
    91‚Üí      (P.card : ‚ĄĚ) ‚Č§ K * (X.card : ‚ĄĚ) ^ ((4 : ‚ĄĚ) / 3) := by
    92‚Üí  sorry
    93‚Üí
    94‚Üíend Erdos956
    95‚Üí

file.content:
/-
Copyright 2026 The Formal Conjectures Authors.

Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
You may obtain a copy of the License at

    https://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0

Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
limitations under the License.
-/

import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports

/-!
# ErdŇĎs Problem 956

*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/956](https://www.erdosproblems.com/956)

If $C, D \subseteq \mathbb{R}^2$ then the distance between $C$ and $D$ is defined by
$\delta(C, D) = \inf \{ \|c - d\| \mid c \in C, d \in D \}$.

Let $h(n)$ be the maximal number of unit distances between disjoint convex
translates. That is, the maximal $m$ such that there is a compact convex set
$C \subset \mathbb{R}^2$ and a set $X$ of size $n$ such that all $(C + x)_{x \in X}$ are disjoint
and there are $m$ pairs $x_1, x_2 \in X$ such that $\delta(C + x_1, C + x_2) = 1$.

[ErPa90] ErdŇĎs, P. and Pach, J.
-/

namespace Erdos956

/-- The distance between two subsets of a metric space:
$\delta(C, D) = \inf \{ \operatorname{dist}(c, d) \mid c \in C, d \in D \}$. -/
noncomputable def setDist956 {őĪ : Type*} [PseudoMetricSpace őĪ]
    (C D : Set őĪ) : ‚ĄĚ :=
  sInf {r : ‚ĄĚ | ‚ąÉ c ‚ąą C, ‚ąÉ d ‚ąą D, r = dist c d}

/-- The translate of a set $C$ by a vector $x$: $C + x = \{ c + x \mid c \in C \}$. -/
def translate956 {őĪ : Type*} [Add őĪ] (C : Set őĪ) (x : őĪ) : Set őĪ :=
  (¬∑ + x) '' C

/--
**ErdŇĎs Problem 956** [ErPa90]:

There exists a constant $c > 0$ such that for all sufficiently large $n$,
one can find a compact convex set $C \subset \mathbb{R}^2$ and $n$ translation vectors whose
translates of $C$ are pairwise disjoint with more than $n^{1+c}$ pairs at
unit set-distance.
-/
@[category research open, AMS 52 5]
theorem erdos_956 :
    ‚ąÉ c : ‚ĄĚ, c > 0 ‚ąß
    ‚ąÉ N‚āÄ : ‚Ąē, ‚ąÄ n : ‚Ąē, n ‚Č• N‚āÄ ‚Üí
      ‚ąÉ (C : Set (EuclideanSpace ‚ĄĚ (Fin 2))),
        IsCompact C ‚ąß Convex ‚ĄĚ C ‚ąß C.Nonempty ‚ąß
      ‚ąÉ (X : Finset (EuclideanSpace ‚ĄĚ (Fin 2))),
        X.card = n ‚ąß
        (‚ąÄ x‚āĀ ‚ąą X, ‚ąÄ x‚āā ‚ąą X, x‚āĀ ‚Č† x‚āā ‚Üí
          Disjoint (translate956 C x‚āĀ) (translate956 C x‚āā)) ‚ąß
      ‚ąÉ (P : Finset (EuclideanSpace ‚ĄĚ (Fin 2) √ó EuclideanSpace ‚ĄĚ (Fin 2))),
        (‚ąÄ p ‚ąą P, p.1 ‚ąą X ‚ąß p.2 ‚ąą X ‚ąß p.1 ‚Č† p.2 ‚ąß
          setDist956 (translate956 C p.1) (translate956 C p.2) = 1) ‚ąß
        (‚ąÄ p ‚ąą P, (p.2, p.1) ‚ąČ P) ‚ąß
        ((P.card : ‚ĄĚ) > (n : ‚ĄĚ) ^ ((1 : ‚ĄĚ) + c)) := by
  sorry

/--
**ErdŇĎs Problem 956, upper bound** [ErPa90]:

$h(n) \ll n^{4/3}$. For any compact convex set $C$ and $n$ translations with
pairwise disjoint translates, the number of pairs at unit set-distance
is $O(n^{4/3})$.
-/
@[category research solved, AMS 52 5]
theorem erdos_956.variants.upper_bound :
    ‚ąÉ K : ‚ĄĚ, K > 0 ‚ąß
    ‚ąÄ (C : Set (EuclideanSpace ‚ĄĚ (Fin 2))),
      IsCompact C ‚Üí Convex ‚ĄĚ C ‚Üí C.Nonempty ‚Üí
    ‚ąÄ (X : Finset (EuclideanSpace ‚ĄĚ (Fin 2))),
      (‚ąÄ x‚āĀ ‚ąą X, ‚ąÄ x‚āā ‚ąą X, x‚āĀ ‚Č† x‚āā ‚Üí
        Disjoint (translate956 C x‚āĀ) (translate956 C x‚āā)) ‚Üí
    ‚ąÄ (P : Finset (EuclideanSpace ‚ĄĚ (Fin 2) √ó EuclideanSpace ‚ĄĚ (Fin 2))),
      (‚ąÄ p ‚ąą P, p.1 ‚ąą X ‚ąß p.2 ‚ąą X ‚ąß p.1 ‚Č† p.2 ‚ąß
        setDist956 (translate956 C p.1) (translate956 C p.2) = 1) ‚Üí
      (‚ąÄ p ‚ąą P, (p.2, p.1) ‚ąČ P) ‚Üí
      (P.card : ‚ĄĚ) ‚Č§ K * (X.card : ‚ĄĚ) ^ ((4 : ‚ĄĚ) / 3) := by
  sorry

end Erdos956

/-!
# ErdŇĎs Problem 956

*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/956](https://www.erdosproblems.com/956)

If $C, D \subseteq \mathbb{R}^2$ then the distance between $C$ and $D$ is defined by
$\delta(C, D) = \inf \{ \|c - d\| \mid c \in C, d \in D \}$.

Let $h(n)$ be the maximal number of unit distances between disjoint convex
translates. That is, the maximal $m$ such that there is a compact convex set
$C \subset \mathbb{R}^2$ and a set $X$ of size $n$ such that all $(C + x)_{x \in X}$ are disjoint
and there are $m$ pairs $x_1, x_2 \in X$ such that $\delta(C + x_1, C + x_2) = 1$.

-/

/-- The distance between two subsets of a metric space:
$\delta(C, D) = \inf \{ \operatorname{dist}(c, d) \mid c \in C, d \in D \}$. -/

    (C D : Set őĪ) : ‚ĄĚ :=
  sInf {r : ‚ĄĚ | ‚ąÉ c ‚ąą C, ‚ąÉ d ‚ąą D, r = dist c d}

/-- The translate of a set $C$ by a vector $x$: $C + x = \{ c + x \mid c \in C \}$. -/

  (¬∑ + x) '' C

/--
**ErdŇĎs Problem 956** [ErPa90]:

There exists a constant $c > 0$ such that for all sufficiently large $n$,

translates of $C$ are pairwise disjoint with more than $n^{1+c}$ pairs at
unit set-distance.

theorem erdos_956 :
    ‚ąÉ c : ‚ĄĚ, c > 0 ‚ąß
    ‚ąÉ N‚āÄ : ‚Ąē, ‚ąÄ n : ‚Ąē, n ‚Č• N‚āÄ ‚Üí
      ‚ąÉ (C : Set (EuclideanSpace ‚ĄĚ (Fin 2))),
        IsCompact C ‚ąß Convex ‚ĄĚ C ‚ąß C.Nonempty ‚ąß
      ‚ąÉ (X : Finset (EuclideanSpace ‚ĄĚ (Fin 2))),
        X.card = n ‚ąß
        (‚ąÄ x‚āĀ ‚ąą X, ‚ąÄ x‚āā ‚ąą X, x‚āĀ ‚Č† x‚āā ‚Üí
          Disjoint (translate956 C x‚āĀ) (translate956 C x‚āā)) ‚ąß
      ‚ąÉ (P : Finset (EuclideanSpace ‚ĄĚ (Fin 2) √ó EuclideanSpace ‚ĄĚ (Fin 2))),
        (‚ąÄ p ‚ąą P, p.1 ‚ąą X ‚ąß p.2 ‚ąą X ‚ąß p.1 ‚Č† p.2 ‚ąß
          setDist956 (translate956 C p.1) (translate956 C p.2) = 1) ‚ąß
        (‚ąÄ p ‚ąą P, (p.2, p.1) ‚ąČ P) ‚ąß
        ((P.card : ‚ĄĚ) > (n : ‚ĄĚ) ^ ((1 : ‚ĄĚ) + c)) := by
  sorry

/--
**ErdŇĎs Problem 956, upper bound** [ErPa90]:

$h(n) \ll n^{4/3}$. For any compact convex set $C$ and $n$ translations with
pairwise disjoint translates, the number of pairs at unit set-distance
is $O(n^{4/3})$.
-/
@[category research solved, AMS 52 5]
theorem erdos_956.variants.upper_bound :
    ‚ąÉ K : ‚ĄĚ, K > 0 ‚ąß
    ‚ąÄ (C : Set (EuclideanSpace ‚ĄĚ (Fin 2))),
      IsCompact C ‚Üí Convex ‚ĄĚ C ‚Üí C.Nonempty ‚Üí
    ‚ąÄ (X : Finset (EuclideanSpace ‚ĄĚ (Fin 2))),
      (‚ąÄ x‚āĀ ‚ąą X, ‚ąÄ x‚āā ‚ąą X, x‚āĀ ‚Č† x‚āā ‚Üí
        Disjoint (translate956 C x‚āĀ) (translate956 C x‚āā)) ‚Üí
    ‚ąÄ (P : Finset (EuclideanSpace ‚ĄĚ (Fin 2) √ó EuclideanSpace ‚ĄĚ (Fin 2))),

      (‚ąÄ p ‚ąą P, (p.2, p.1) ‚ąČ P) ‚Üí
